title: Propositional and First Order Logic
description: Truth tables, logical connectives, quantifiers, and logical equivalences.

title: Sets, Relations, and Functions
description: Set operations, relation properties, partial orders, lattices, and function types.

title: Monoids and Groups
description: Algebraic structures from groupoids to Abelian groups with Lagrange's theorem.

title: Graph Theory
description: Connectivity, matching, coloring, Euler and Hamilton paths.

title: Combinatorics
description: Counting principles, recurrence relations, and generating functions.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Matrices, determinants, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and LU decomposition.

title: Calculus
description: Limits, continuity, differentiability, maxima/minima, MVT, and integration.

title: Probability and Statistics
description: Distributions, Bayes theorem, random variables, mean, median, and standard deviation.

title: Boolean Algebra
description: Boolean laws, canonical forms, K-maps, and Quine-McCluskey minimization.

title: Combinational Circuits
description: Adders, multiplexers, decoders, encoders, and comparators.

title: Sequential Circuits
description: Flip-flops, counters, registers, and state diagrams.

title: Number Representations and Computer Arithmetic
description: Binary systems, signed numbers, fixed/floating point, and IEEE 754.

title: Machine Instructions and Addressing Modes
description: Instruction formats, addressing modes, RISC vs CISC architectures.

title: ALU, Datapath and Control Unit
description: ALU design, datapath components, hardwired vs microprogrammed control.

title: Instruction Pipelining
description: Pipeline stages, hazards, forwarding, stalling, and branch prediction.

title: Memory Hierarchy
description: Cache mapping, replacement policies, write policies, and AMAT.

title: I/O Interface
description: Programmed I/O, interrupt-driven I/O, and DMA modes.

title: Programming in C
description: Pointers, dynamic memory, structures, recursion, and function pointers.

title: Arrays, Stacks, and Queues
description: Array addressing, stack applications, circular queues, and priority queues.

title: Linked Lists
description: Singly, doubly, and circular linked lists with operations and complexity.

title: Trees and Heaps
description: Binary trees, BST, AVL trees, and binary heaps with traversals.

title: Graphs as Data Structure
description: Adjacency matrix/list, BFS, DFS, topological sort, and applications.

title: Searching, Sorting, and Hashing
description: All sorting algorithms compared, binary search, and hash collision handling.

title: Asymptotic Complexity Analysis
description: Big-O, Omega, Theta notation, Master theorem, and amortized analysis.

title: Greedy Algorithms
description: Activity selection, Huffman coding, fractional knapsack, and job sequencing.

title: Dynamic Programming
description: Memoization, tabulation, knapsack, LCS, matrix chain, and Floyd-Warshall.

title: Divide and Conquer
description: Merge sort, quick sort, Strassen's multiplication, and Master theorem.

title: Graph Algorithms
description: MST (Kruskal, Prim), shortest paths (Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford), and SCC.

title: Regular Languages and Finite Automata
description: DFA, NFA, regular expressions, pumping lemma, and closure properties.

title: Context-Free Languages and Pushdown Automata
description: CFG, parse trees, CNF, PDA, and pumping lemma for CFLs.

title: Turing Machines and Undecidability
description: TM variants, Church-Turing thesis, halting problem, and Rice's theorem.

title: Lexical Analysis
description: Tokens, patterns, lexemes, regex-based scanning, and symbol tables.

title: Parsing (Syntax Analysis)
description: LL(1), LR parsers, FIRST/FOLLOW sets, and shift-reduce parsing.

title: Syntax-Directed Translation and Intermediate Code
description: SDDs, runtime environments, activation records, and three-address code.

title: Code Optimization and Data Flow Analysis
description: Constant propagation, liveness analysis, and common subexpression elimination.

title: Processes and Threads
description: Process states, system calls, threads, and inter-process communication.

title: Concurrency and Synchronization
description: Critical sections, semaphores, monitors, and classical sync problems.

title: Deadlock
description: Deadlock conditions, prevention, avoidance with Banker's algorithm, and detection.

title: CPU and I/O Scheduling
description: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, priority scheduling, and disk scheduling algorithms.

title: Memory Management and Virtual Memory
description: Paging, segmentation, demand paging, page replacement, and thrashing.

title: File Systems
description: File allocation methods, directory structures, and free space management.

title: ER Model
description: Entities, attributes, relationships, weak entities, and ER-to-relational mapping.

title: Relational Model and SQL
description: Relational algebra, tuple calculus, SQL queries, joins, and aggregates.

title: Normalization and Integrity Constraints
description: Functional dependencies, 1NF through BCNF, and lossless decomposition.

title: Indexing, B-Trees, and Transactions
description: B/B+ trees, ACID properties, 2PL concurrency control, and serializability.

title: Network Layers and Switching
description: OSI vs TCP/IP models, packet/circuit/virtual circuit switching.

title: Data Link Layer
description: Framing, error detection/correction, MAC protocols, and Ethernet bridging.

title: Network Layer and Routing
description: IPv4 addressing, CIDR, routing algorithms, ARP, DHCP, ICMP, and NAT.

title: Transport Layer
description: TCP/UDP, flow control, congestion control, 3-way handshake, and sockets.

title: Application Layer Protocols
description: DNS, HTTP, FTP, SMTP, email architecture, and TLS basics.

description: Complete syllabus overview with section-wise marks distribution and preparation strategy.

description: Top mistakes per section with correct approaches to avoid losing marks.

description: Verbal ability, numerical aptitude, data interpretation, and logical reasoning (15 marks).

description: GCD, modular arithmetic, Euler's totient, Fermat's theorem, and CRT.

title: Set Theory — Advanced Topics
description: Power sets, countability, Cantor's argument, inclusion-exclusion principle.

title: AVL Trees & Red-Black Trees
description: Detailed rotations, insertion/deletion rules, and AVL vs Red-Black comparison.

title: Disk Scheduling Algorithms
description: FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK with worked seek-time calculations.

description: 15+ SQL problems from basic to advanced with solutions and explanations.

description: Step-by-step subnetting, CIDR, VLSM with 10+ solved problems.

description: Substitution, recursion tree, Master theorem with 10+ practice problems.

description: Boolean laws, flip-flop tables, IEEE 754, and K-map rules at a glance.

title: Mathematical Induction and Proof Techniques
description: Direct proof, contrapositive, contradiction, mathematical induction, strong induction, and structural induction with worked examples.

title: Lattices, Partial Orders, and Boolean Algebra
description: Posets, Hasse diagrams, lattice types, distributive and complemented lattices, and Boolean algebra as a lattice.

title: PLDs, ROM, RAM, and Memory Types
description: ROM, PLA, PAL, FPGA structures, SRAM vs DRAM, memory expansion, and address decoding.

title: Microprogrammed Control Unit Design
description: Hardwired vs microprogrammed control, horizontal/vertical microinstructions, sequencing, and nano-programming.

title: Parallel Processing and Multiprocessor Systems
description: Flynn's taxonomy, cache coherence (MESI), Amdahl's Law, interconnection networks, and SMP architecture.

title: OS Structure, System Calls, and Kernel Design
description: System call types, monolithic vs microkernel vs modular OS, user/kernel mode, and virtual machines.

title: Real-Time Scheduling and Advanced CPU Scheduling
description: RMS, EDF, multiprocessor scheduling, Linux CFS, priority inversion, and priority inheritance.

title: Query Processing and Optimization
description: Join algorithms, external sorting, cost estimation, heuristic and cost-based query optimization.

title: Recovery Systems and Log-Based Recovery
description: WAL protocol, undo/redo operations, checkpointing, ARIES recovery, and shadow paging.

title: Advanced Concurrency Control in DBMS
description: 2PL variants, timestamp ordering, MVCC, multiple granularity locking, and deadlock handling schemes.

title: Network Security Fundamentals
description: RSA, Diffie-Hellman, digital signatures, SSL/TLS handshake, firewalls, and IPSec protocols.

title: TCP Congestion Control — Deep Dive
description: Slow start, congestion avoidance, fast retransmit/recovery, TCP Tahoe vs Reno vs CUBIC with numerical examples.

title: NP-Completeness and Computational Complexity
description: P, NP, NP-hard, NP-complete classes, Cook-Levin theorem, reductions, and key NPC problems.

title: String Matching Algorithms
description: Naive, Rabin-Karp, KMP prefix table, Boyer-Moore, and finite automaton matchers with worked examples.

title: Backtracking and Branch & Bound
description: N-Queens, graph coloring, 0/1 knapsack, TSP with state space trees and bounding functions.

title: Closure Properties and Decision Problems
description: Closure under operations for Regular, CFL, Recursive, RE languages and decidability of key problems.

title: Complexity Classes — P, NP, PSPACE, and Beyond
description: P, NP, co-NP, PSPACE, Savitch's theorem, hierarchy theorems, and Karp vs Turing reductions.
